section_title: Where an agent, without having authority to do so, has appointed a
content:
193. Where an agent, without having authority to do so, has appointed a
responsibility
person to act as a sub-agent, the agent stands towards such person in the
for sub-
agent relation of a principal to an agent, and is responsible for his acts both to the
appointed
principal and to third persons; the principal is not represented by or
without
authority responsible for the acts of the person so employed, nor is that person
responsible to the principal.

Relation
